title: fix(code-review): unify on PullRequest shape, converge on org-scoped storage
rationale: foundational refactor, multi-round reviews, mix of TRUE/FALSE verdicts
body:
## Summary

The code-review dashboard rendered empty because the CRREAD-1 refactor split writes (org-scoped) from reads (top-level `pullRequests/`) and never finished bridging them. The webhook wrote `organizations/{orgId}/pullRequests/{prId}` while reads queried top-level `pullRequests/{prId}` — which nothing ever wrote. MCP `record_review_started` / `record_finding` / `record_review_completed` wrote review and finding subcollections under the dead top-level path, so every review the executor produced was orphaned and invisible to the UI.

This PR converges on a single tree under `organizations/{orgId}/pullRequests/{prId}` and a single doc shape (`PullRequest`); drops `CodeReviewPullRequest` entirely. Every read returns the canonical `PullRequest` directly — no more parallel projection type to drift.

## What changed

**Storage**
- Review/finding subcollections moved to `organizations/{orgId}/pullRequests/{prId}/reviews/{reviewId}[/findings/{id}]`.
- All read methods (`getPullRequest`, `listPullRequests`, `getReview`, `getReviews`, `getFinding`, `getFindings`, `getAllFindings`, `patchFinding`, `listFindingsByReview`) take `orgId`. Cross-tenant access is now path-scoped — impossible by construction, so the route-layer `pr.orgId !== orgId` IDOR check is removed.
- `listPullRequests` filters via nested fields (`repository.fullName`, `author.username`); composite indexes updated with `COLLECTION_GROUP` scope so the same indexes serve every org's subcollection.

**Denormalisation** (so the list view doesn't fan out per row)
- `recordFinding` bumps the parent PR's `hasFindingsMin` if the new finding's severity beats the current value, atomically in the same transaction.
- `completeReview` increments `stats.reviewCount`, sets `latestReviewId`, and recomputes `hasFindingsMin` from the review's persisted findings.

**UI**
- `CodeReviewPullsPage` / `CodeReviewPullDetailPage` consume `PullRequest` directly: `pr.repository.fullName`, `pr.author?.username`, `pr.stats.reviewCount`. No more parallel projection type.

**Backfill**
- `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts` walks the orphaned top-level `pullRequests/{prId}/reviews` and `.../findings` docs, copies them to the org-scoped tree, and recomputes the parent PR's denormalised fields. Idempotent. Pass `--apply` to write, `--apply --delete-source` to clean up the legacy tree once verified.

## Deploy order

1. Merge + ship server changes (writes go to org-scoped tree, reads come from it).
2. Run `npx tsx scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts` (dry-run) to inspect what would move.
3. Run with `--apply` to actually copy historical data + recompute denormalised fields.
4. Verify the dashboard renders the historical PR (e.g. https://app.aictrl.dev/code-review/pulls/ce6d4f9e-3106-44bc-a9a7-ca380bb0bbb3-aictrl-dev-aictrl-1781 should now show its 2 reviews + 8 findings).
5. Rerun with `--apply --delete-source` once the migration is confirmed.

## Test plan

- [x] `test/code-review/pull-request-manager.test.ts` (32 tests) — manager unit
- [x] `test/code-review-routes.test.ts` (24 tests) — pulls list + detail routes
- [x] `test/code-review/finding-actions-routes.test.ts` (42 tests) — finding actions + IDOR
- [x] `test/mcp/code-review.test.ts` (47 tests) — MCP record_* tools
- [x] `test/api/github-app-routes.test.ts` — webhook upsertPR + publishers
- [x] `test/pull-request-output-handler.test.ts` (13 tests) — output handler
- [x] `test/scripts/backfill-pull-requests.test.ts` — existing backfill script unaffected
- [x] UI: `CodeReviewPullsPage.test.tsx`, `CodeReviewPullDetailPage.test.tsx` (16 tests)
- [x] `tsc --noEmit` clean on both server and ui projects
- [x] `npm run lint` clean on both
- [x] `npm run check:types-mirror` — PullRequest stays mirrored between server and ui
- [x] `ast-grep scan`, `npm run check:knip`, `npm run check:edge-coverage` — clean
- [ ] Manual: hit `/code-review/pulls` and `/code-review/pulls/:prId` after backfill against sandbox

## Notes

- The unrelated test failures elsewhere on `sandbox` (admin-audit, planner-commit, kg-query, task-template-bootstrap, etc.) pre-exist this PR — confirmed by running them on the unmodified `sandbox` worktree.
- Two redundant Firestore indexes on the dead top-level `pullRequests` collection are removed (`orgId+openedAt+id` and `state+openedAt`) — the org-scoped subcollection's path-based isolation makes the `orgId` field index obsolete.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

botReviews.body:
## Code Review — PR #1785

**fix(code-review): unify on PullRequest shape, converge on org-scoped storage**

5 MAJOR · 8 MINOR · 1 NIT

---

### MAJOR

| # | File | Finding |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:1066` | **recordFinding writes undefined Firestore field values** — `startLine`, `endLine`, and `suggestion` use `?? undefined`. Firestore rejects undefined field values unless `ignoreUndefinedProperties` is enabled. `patchFinding` strips undefined values before writing, showing the codebase is aware of this constraint, but `recordFinding` does not. Replace `?? undefined` with `?? null`. |
| 2 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:1000` | **createReview writes undefined Firestore field values** — `executionId` and `skillId` use `?? undefined`. Same inconsistency as above — `patchFinding` strips undefined, but `createReview` does not. Use `?? null` or conditionally include the fields. |
| 3 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:718` | **listPullRequests filters need composite Firestore indexes** — The repo filter queries `repository.fullName` and author filter queries `author.username`. Combined with `orderBy('openedAt', 'desc').orderBy('id', 'desc')`, these require composite indexes for the COLLECTION_GROUP scope. The PR updates `firestore.indexes.json` to COLLECTION_GROUP scope and removes `orgId` but does not add indexes for the new nested field paths with the full sort order. Missing: `(repository.fullName ASC, openedAt DESC, id DESC)` and `(author.username ASC, openedAt DESC, id DESC)`. |
| 4 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:246` | **Unbounded collectionGroup scan loads all reviews into memory** — `db.collectionGroup('reviews').get()` streams every review across every org into a single snapshot with no pagination. On a large production dataset, this can OOM or exceed Firestore response limits. Use cursor-based pagination (`limit(500)` + `startAfter`) or the streaming API. |
| 5 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:196` | **N+1 query pattern in recomputeParentPr** — Each review triggers a separate `findings` subcollection read. A PR with N reviews triggers N+1 Firestore reads. Use `db.getAll()` batching or a scoped `collectionGroup('findings')` query. |

### MINOR

| # | File | Finding |
|---|------|---------|
| 6 | `firestore.indexes.json:3` | **COLLECTION_GROUP indexes lack orgId field (defense-in-depth)** — All three indexes changed to COLLECTION_GROUP scope but dropped `orgId` from the leading field. If a future change introduces a `collectionGroup('pullRequests')` query without an explicit `orgId` where clause, Firestore will return cross-tenant data. Consider keeping `orgId` as the first field for defense-in-depth. |
| 7 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:672` | **Stale comment references top-level collection** — Comment says "top-level pullRequests collection" but all methods now query the org-scoped subcollection. |
| 8 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:1135` | **completeReview reads findings outside transaction** — `findingsSnap` is fetched outside the transaction, so `worstSeverity` may be stale if findings are added concurrently. Safe in practice because `hasFindingsMin` is monotonic (only upgraded) and `recordFinding` independently updates it atomically. Document this invariant explicitly. |
| 9 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:885` | **patchFinding uses non-atomic get-then-update** (pre-existing) — Concurrent writes to the same finding field could cause a lost update. Low risk since action-layer fields are typically set independently. |
| 10 | `test/code-review/finding-actions-routes.test.ts:110` | **BASE_PR mock uses old flat PullRequest shape** — Uses `repoFullName`, `author` as string, `reviewCount` at top level instead of nested `repository`, `author`, `stats` objects. Tests pass because routes only null-check the PR, but future code accessing nested fields would silently break. |
| 11 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:112` | **Individual Firestore writes instead of batched writes** — Each finding/review is written individually. Using `db.batch()` would coalesce up to 500 operations per commit, reducing round-trips. |
| 12 | `ui/src/pages/code-review/CodeReviewPullsPage.tsx:70` | **pr.repository.fullName without optional chaining** — During migration rollout, stale PRs may lack the nested `repository` object. `author` is correctly guarded with `?.username` but `repository` is not. Use `pr.repository?.fullName ?? '—'`. |
| 13 | `ui/src/pages/code-review/CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx:84` | **pr.repository.fullName without optional chaining** — Same issue as above. Use `pr.repository?.fullName ?? '—'`. |

### NIT

| # | File | Finding |
|---|------|---------|
| 14 | `ui/src/pages/code-review/__tests__/CodeReviewPullsPage.test.tsx:67` | **MOCK_PR_2 inherits stale url** — Spreads MOCK_PR (url ends in `/pull/42`) but overrides number to 43. Add `url: '...pull/43'` to the override. |

---

Reviewed SHA: da25451a5f65881dd1398addcfeb49f35257140f

## Code Review — PR #1785

**Subagents:** Security, Consistency, Bug-Hunter (2 shards)  
**Findings:** 1 BLOCKER · 2 MAJOR · 7 MINOR

---

### BLOCKER

| # | File | Line | Rule | Summary |
|---|------|------|------|---------|
| 1 | `firestore.indexes.json` | 5–85 | bug/index-scope-mismatch | COLLECTION_GROUP indexes won't serve COLLECTION queries |

**#1 — COLLECTION_GROUP indexes won't serve COLLECTION queries**
`firestore.indexes.json:5`

All four `pullRequests` composite indexes use `"queryScope": "COLLECTION_GROUP"`, but every query in `PullRequestManager.listPullRequests()` uses `this.prCollection(orgId)` which resolves to `db.collection('organizations').doc(orgId).collection('pullRequests')` — a **COLLECTION-scoped** query. Firestore will NOT use `COLLECTION_GROUP`-scoped indexes for `COLLECTION`-scoped queries. In production, `listPullRequests` will fail with a "missing index" error for any query requiring a composite index (e.g. filtering by state, repo, or author). The emulator auto-creates indexes so tests pass, masking this issue.

**Fix:** Change all four `pullRequests` indexes back to `"queryScope": "COLLECTION"`. Alternatively, refactor `listPullRequests` to use `db.collectionGroup('pullRequests')` queries, which would match `COLLECTION_GROUP` indexes. The code comment at `pull-request-manager.ts:698` acknowledges this is designed for a future `collectionGroup` migration, but the current code hasn't been migrated yet.

---

### MAJOR

| # | File | Line | Rule | Summary |
|---|------|------|------|---------|
| 2 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts` | 115 | bug/dry-run-counter-misleading | Dry-run reports findings as 'copied' when no writes occurred |
| 3 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts` | 148–156 | bug/non-atomic-delete | deleteSourceTree is non-atomic; partial failure orphans data |

**#2 — Dry-run reports findings as 'copied' when no writes occurred**
`scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:115`

`copyReview` returns `{copied: true}` even when `APPLY` is false, and `copyFindings` increments `stats.findingsCopied` outside the `if (APPLY)` guard. In dry-run mode the summary prints "N findings copied" and "N reviews copied" even though nothing was written. An operator trusting the dry-run output could proceed to `--delete-source` on data that was never actually copied, causing data loss.

**Fix:** Guard the return value and counter increments on `APPLY`: return `{copied: APPLY, alreadyAtTarget: false}` from `copyReview`, and move `stats.findingsCopied++` inside the `if (APPLY)` block. Alternatively, add separate "would_copy" counters for dry-run mode.

**#3 — deleteSourceTree is non-atomic; partial failure orphans data**
`scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:148–156`

`deleteSourceTree` deletes findings one-by-one then deletes the review doc. If the process crashes or the function throws mid-way through finding deletion, some findings are gone while the review doc and remaining findings persist. A re-run would skip the review ("already at target") but never re-copy the deleted source findings — they're lost. A batch delete or at minimum deleting the review doc FIRST (so re-run re-copies everything) would be safer.

**Fix:** Use `db.batch()` to delete all findings + the review doc atomically, or delete the review doc first so a re-run re-copies all findings from source (since the review would no longer exist at source to skip).

---

### MINOR

| # | File | Line | Rule | Summary |
|---|------|------|------|---------|
| 4 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts` | 100–106 | consistency/path-duplication | Hardcoded Firestore path strings duplicate manager constants |
| 5 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts` | 195 | bug/fragile-id-access | recomputeParentPr uses review.id from data, not doc key |
| 6 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts` | 200–212 | performance/unbounded-finding-fanout | recomputeParentPr fans out finding reads for all reviews in parallel |
| 7 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts` | 237 | bug/dry-run-counter-misleading | parentPrsRecomputed counter increments in dry-run mode |
| 8 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts` | 567–571 | consistency/missing-id-injection | getPR returns raw data without injecting snap.id as fallback |
| 9 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts` | 1157–1183 | bug/stale-read-outside-tx | completeReview computes findings stats outside transaction |
| 10 | `server/services/pull-request-manager.ts` | 1227–1228 | consistency/denormalised-field-update-style | Mixed dot-notation and top-level field update style |

**#4 — Hardcoded Firestore path strings duplicate manager constants**
`scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:100–106`

The backfill script hardcodes `'organizations'`, `'pullRequests'`, `'reviews'`, `'findings'` as path segments, duplicating the `ORGS_COLLECTION`, `PRS_SUBCOLLECTION`, `REVIEWS_SUBCOLLECTION`, `FINDINGS_SUBCOLLECTION` constants in `pull-request-manager.ts`. If those constants ever change, the backfill would silently write to the wrong paths.

**Fix:** Export the path constants from `pull-request-manager.ts` and import them in the backfill script, or extract them to a shared constants module.

**#5 — recomputeParentPr uses review.id from data, not doc key**
`scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:195`

Reads `review.id` from document data (`reviewDoc.data() as CodeReview`) rather than using `reviewDoc.id` (the Firestore document key). If any pre-existing review document was written by an older code version that did not include an `id` field inside the document body, `latestReviewId` would be set to `undefined`.

**Fix:** Replace `review.id` with `reviewDoc.id`.

**#6 — recomputeParentPr fans out finding reads for all reviews in parallel**
`scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:200–212`

Fetches ALL findings for ALL reviews under a PR in a single `Promise.all`. For a PR with many reviews each containing many findings, this creates an unbounded number of concurrent Firestore reads. While ~10 docs in prod, there's no safety cap.

**Fix:** Add a cap on the number of reviews processed (similar to `MAX_REVIEWS_PER_PR` in `PullRequestManager`), or process reviews sequentially with bounded concurrency.

**#7 — parentPrsRecomputed counter increments in dry-run mode**
`scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:237`

`stats.parentPrsRecomputed++` is outside the `if (APPLY)` guard. In dry-run mode the summary reports N recomputed parents even though no writes occurred.

**Fix:** Move `stats.parentPrsRecomputed++` inside the `if (APPLY)` block.

**#8 — getPR returns raw data without injecting snap.id as fallback**
`server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:567–571`

`getPR` returns `snap.data() as PullRequest` directly, while `getPullRequest` uses the `toPullRequest` helper which injects `{ id: snap.id, ...data }`. If a document was created without an `id` field in its data, `getPR` would return a `PullRequest` with a missing/undefined `id`.

**Fix:** Use the `toPullRequest(snap)` helper in `getPR` as well.

**#9 — completeReview computes findings stats outside transaction**
`server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:1157–1183`

Reads the findings collection outside the transaction, computes `findingCount`/`findingsBySeverity`, then writes those values inside the transaction. If `recordFinding` appends a new finding between the pre-tx snapshot and the tx commit, the review document's stats will be permanently inaccurate until the next re-review. The `hasFindingsMin` monotonic guard prevents downgrade of that field, but the review doc's own stats can under-count.

**Fix:** Consider re-reading findings inside the transaction, or document that `findingCount` is a point-in-time snapshot and may under-report by the number of concurrently recorded findings.

**#10 — Mixed dot-notation and top-level field update style**
`server/services/pull-request-manager.ts:1227–1228`

In `completeReview`, `'stats.reviewCount'` uses Firestore dot-notation for a nested field, while `latestReviewId` and `hasFindingsMin` use top-level notation. Both work correctly, but the inconsistency makes the denormalisation pattern harder to audit.

---

Reviewed SHA: ea0b53dd6d0f3f25561a0950d1dd673765621bf6

## Code Review: PR #1785

**Converge on org-scoped Firestore tree, remove CodeReviewPullRequest type**

1 MAJOR · 7 MINOR · 2 NIT

---

### MAJOR

| # | File | Finding |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:295` | **Backfill skips reviews missing `id` in doc body** — The main loop reads `review.id` from the document body, but `recomputeParentPr` (line 207) notes legacy reviews may lack this field and falls back to `reviewDoc.id`. If a legacy review's body has no `id`, the `!reviewId` guard skips it — data is never migrated. Fix: `const reviewId = review.id \|\| reviewDoc.id;` |

### MINOR

| # | File | Finding |
|---|------|---------|
| 2 | `server/api/code-review-routes.ts:90` | **Missing orgId format validation** — The list handler passes raw `req.params.orgId` to Firestore without the `isValidParam()` guard used in sibling `code-review-findings-routes.ts`. Defence-in-depth: add the same validation. |
| 3 | `server/api/code-review-routes.ts:208` | **orgId unvalidated in detail handler** — Same as #2: `prId` is validated but `orgId` is not before Firestore path construction. |
| 4 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:150` | **deleteSourceTree can exceed Firestore 500-op batch** — All finding deletes + review doc delete are batched together. If a review has 499+ findings the batch throws. Low risk today (~10 docs per PR) but no guard exists. |
| 5 | `scripts/backfill-pr-orgscoped.ts:331` | **Fragile `::` delimiter split** — `touchedPrs` keys use `${orgId}::${prId}`, split on `::` to recover parts. If either ID ever contained `::`, destructuring silently drops trailing segments. Consider a `Map<string, {orgId, prId}>` instead. |
| 6 | `ui/src/pages/code-review/CodeReviewPullDetailPage.tsx:84` | **`pr.repository.fullName` without null guard** — Other nullable fields got `??` fallbacks in this PR but `repository.fullName` has no optional chaining. If API returns partial data, render crashes. Fix: `pr.repository?.fullName ?? '—'`. |
| 7 | `ui/src/pages/code-review/CodeReviewPullsPage.tsx:70` | **Same null-safety issue in list row** — `pr.repository.fullName` accessed without `?.`. A partial response crashes the entire pulls list render. |
| 8 | `test/code-review/finding-actions-routes.test.ts:143` | **Inconsistent test fixture** — `BASE_PR` has `stats.reviewCount: 1` but `latestReviewId: null`, an impossible state. Won't break existing assertions but could mask bugs. |

### NIT

| # | File | Finding |
|---|------|---------|
| 9 | `server/api/code-review-findings-routes.ts:37` | **Stale IDOR comment** — Header says "prId.orgId checked against URL :orgId" but the explicit check was removed in favour of path-scoped reads. Update comment to reflect new model. |
| 10 | `server/api/code-review-routes.ts:21` | **Stale auth comment** — Says "checkOrgMember against resource.orgId" but code no longer compares `resource.orgId`. Update to mention path-scoped Firestore reads. |

---

Reviewed SHA: b2814bd40865f8fd194842279d963e21b04bc88b

labelled.reason: verified — prCollection(orgId) is COLLECTION-scoped; index scope must match

labelled.description: copyReview returns copied:true and findingsCopied++ runs in dry-run — operator could trust dry-run output and proceed to --delete-source on data that was never copied
labelled.reason: verified — both counters and return value were unguarded

labelled.description: deleteSourceTree deletes findings one-by-one then deletes review doc; partial failure orphans data
labelled.reason: data-loss claim is inaccurate (copyFindings runs before deleteSourceTree at line 299), but batch atomicity is a small genuine robustness improvement; fixed via db.batch()

labelled.description: hardcoded path strings duplicate manager constants
labelled.reason: one-shot migration script, retired post-run; not worth the churn

labelled.description: recomputeParentPr uses review.id from data instead of reviewDoc.id
labelled.reason: backfill processes legacy data — doc key is the right source of truth

labelled.description: recomputeParentPr fans out finding reads with no concurrency cap
labelled.reason: one-shot script, ~10 docs in prod per bot's own admission — no real risk

labelled.description: parentPrsRecomputed counter increments outside APPLY guard
labelled.reason: same bug class as [2]; fixed together

labelled.description: getPR returns raw data without injecting snap.id as fallback
labelled.reason: trivial fix — switched to toPullRequest(snap) helper that getPullRequest already uses

labelled.description: completeReview computes findings stats outside transaction; concurrent recordFinding can produce stale findingCount
labelled.reason: design intent already documented at lines 1140-1156 with the exact trade-off bot raises; suggested fix would regress clarity

labelled.description: mixed dot-notation and top-level field update style
labelled.reason: mixing required by Firestore — dot-notation prevents overwriting nested objects via shallow merge; cannot be made consistent without changing data shape
